text: ni ewole̱ gbe̱i ake̱ Noa, ni ekē̱: Mone̱ baše̱dše wo̱mī ye̱ wo̱nitšume̱i ke̱ wo̱nidšiaṅ to̱le̱i le̱ ni dše̱ šikpoṅ le̱ *lōmo̱ ni Iehowa elomo̱le̱ le̱ bewo̱ le̱ mli. *
translated: and he spoke these words to Noah, saying: You and your wife and your sons and your daughters-in-law should come out of the ark because Jehovah has commanded that you come out.

ref: Store up enough food both for yourself and for them.
text: Ni bo le̱, ṅõ niyenii fẽ ní ayeo̱ le̱ eko ke̱-ba oṅō, ni obna nā ohã bodientše̱ ke̱ame̱ yeli.
translated: If you do this, then when your children ask you tomorrow what this means, you will tell them the story of what happened.

ref: Seven days later a flood began to cover the earth.
text: Ni eba mli ake̱ gbĩĩ kpawo le̱ se̱ le̱ nu afua le̱ ba šikpoṅ le̱ no̱.
translated: When you go to the farm, you should take food along with you.

ref: A hundred fifty days later, the water started going down.
text: Ni nni le̱ damo̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, gbĩĩ oha ke̱ nyoṅmai-enumo̱.
translated: When you are in a state of confusion, seek wise counsel.

ref: It returned in the evening, holding in its beak a green leaf from an olive tree. Noah knew the water was finally going down.
text: Ni okpo le̱ ba eṅõ gbe̱ke̱nãši, ni na, mũtšo-bāfo̱ kõ enā; ni Noa ná le ake̱ nui le̱ emī̃ ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱.
translated: And when the waters had dried up from the earth, he said, the animals shall go out; and Noah went and opened the door of the ark.

ref: I promise every living creature that the earth and those living on it will never again be destroyed by a flood.
text: Ni mahã mikpãṅmo̱ ní mike̱nye ekpãṅ le̱ amã ši, ni nu afua le̱ ebaplenẽṅ helo fẽ doṅṅ, ni asaṅ nu afua ko ebakpãtãṅ šikpoṅ le̱ hĩe gbĩkogbĩko.
translated: When you want to buy something and you don't have enough money, you borrow money from someone or you save money little by little.

ref: The rainbow that I have put in the sky will be my sign to you and to every living creature on earth. It will remind you that I will keep this promise forever.
text: Miṅõ mikwãbue̱te̱ mito atatui le̱ amli, ni efe kpāṅmo̱ ni yo̱ mike̱ šikpoṅ le̱ teṅ le̱ he okadi.
translated: When we were still children, our father used to tell us stories in the evening at home.

ref: Shem and Japheth put a robe over their shoulders and walked backwards into the tent. Without looking at their father, they placed it over his body.
text: Ši Šem ke̱ Iafet ko̱ mama ni ame̱ṅõ-fõ ame̱yi enyo̱ le̱ fẽ ame̱kõdši ano̱, ni ame̱nyie se̱se̱, ni ame̱te ni ame̱ke̱-yaha ame̱tše̱ yayaiaṅ; ši ame̱tšṍmo̱ ame̱hĩeaṅ ame̱hã se̱gbe̱, ni ame̱naa ame̱tše̱ yayaiaṅ.
translated: Shem, Ham and Japheth took a garment and laid it across their shoulders; then they walked in backward and covered their father's naked body. Their faces were turned the other way so that they would not see their father naked.

ref: I pray God will give Japheth more and more land and let him take over the territory of Shem. May Canaan be his slave.”
text: Nyoṅmo̱ aabā Iafet mli alē̱, ni *eehī Šem bũi le̱ amli, ni Kanaan aatšṍ ame̱-nyṍṅ! *Ef.
translated: May God enlarge Japheth, and let him dwell in the tents of Shem, and let Canaan be his servant! *Eph.

ref: Japheth's descendants had their own languages, tribes, and land. They were Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras. Gomer was the ancestor of Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah. Javan was the ancestor of Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Dodanim, who settled along the coast. The Descendants of Ham
text: Mene̱mei amli mei dše̱ ſã dšeṅmādši le̱ aṅšo̱kpo̱i le̱ ano̱, ye̱ ame̱šikpõdši le̱ ano̱, mofẽmo ke̱ ewiemo̱, ye̱ ame̱kutšei ke̱ ame̱dšeṅmādši le̱ anā. Zak.
translated: And I will pour out on the house of David and on the inhabitants of Jerusalem, the spirit of grace and of supplication, and they shall look on me whom they have pierced. Zech.

ref: This completes the list of Noah's descendants. After the flood their descendants became nations and spread all over the world.
text: Mene̱mei dši Noa bii le̱ awekui ye̱ ame̱sešibii le̱ anã ye̱ ame̱mãdši le̱ amli; ni mene̱mei amli dšeṅmãdši le̱ dše̱ fã šikpoṅ le̱ ṅo̱ ye̱ nu afua le̱ se̱.
translated: And Noah built an altar to the Lord and took some of every clean animal and every clean bird and offered burnt offerings on the altar.

text: Ni beni Šem fo̱ Arpakšad se̱ le̱, eye afii ohai-enumo̱, ni efo̱ bihĩĩ ke̱ biyei.

ref: Peleg had more children and died at the age of

translated: When Reu had lived thirty years, he became the father of Serug, and he had other sons and daughters.

ref: Please save my life by saying you are my sister.”
text: Nohewo̱ le̱, mikpao fai, kē̱mo̱ ake̱ minyemiyō dšio, koni ahĩ ahāmi ye̱ ohewo̱, ni misusuma yi aná wala ye̱ ohewo̱.
translated: Therefore, I say to you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat or what you will drink, nor about your body, what you will wear.

ref: The king was good to Abram because of Sarai, and Abram was given sheep, cattle, donkeys, slaves, and camels.
text: Ni ehĩe Abram dšogbāṅ ye̱ ehewo̱, ni ená tōi ke̱ tšinai ke̱ tedšihĩĩ ke̱ we-hĩĩ ke̱ weyei ke̱ tedšiyei ke̱ yomai.
translated: And Abram was very rich in cattle, in silver, in gold, in male servants, in female servants, in camels, and in donkeys.

ref: Why did you make me believe she was your sister? Now I've married her. Take her and go! She's your wife.”
text: Mẽba okē̱ aké̱: Minyemiyō dšile? nto kũlē̱ mibaṅõlē̱ mafe miṅã. Ni agbe̱né̱, na oṅã, ṅõle̱ ni oke̱le̱ aya!
translated: Some people say: Why do you preach? or why do you teach us these things. But truly, I say, if you do not hear, you will perish!

ref: Abram took down his tents and went to live near the sacred trees of Mamre at Hebron, where he built an altar in honor of the Lord.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Abram fā ebū le̱ ni eba ebahī Mamre tšei kutū ni yo̱ Hebron le̱ ašiši, ni emā afo̱lešàlate̱ ye̱ dše̱i ehã Iehowa.
translated: Then Abram moved his tent and went and settled by the oaks of Mamre, which are at Hebron, and there he built an altar to the Lord.

ref: The king of Sodom said to Abram, “All I want are my people. You can keep everything else.”
text: Ni Sodom maṅtše̱ le̱ kē̱ Abram ake̱: Hãmi gbomei le̱, ni oṅõ nii le̱ ohã ohe.
translated: The king of Sodom said to Abram: Give me the people, and take the goods for yourself.

ref: Abram answered: The Lord God Most High made the heavens and the earth. And I have promised him
text: Ni Abram kē̱ Sodom maṅtše̱ le̱ ake̱: Mīwó minine no̱ mītšõ Iehowa ṅwei flōflo Nyoṅmo̱ ni dši ṅwei ke̱ šikpoṅ tše̱ le̱,
translated: And Abram said to the king of Sodom: I have lifted up my hand to the Lord, the most high God, possessor of heaven and earth,

ref: And when birds came down to eat the animals, Abram chased them away.
text: Ni lofõdši lōyelo̱i kpleke ši basõmo̱ gbohĩĩ le̱ ano̱, ni Abram ſeame̱.
translated: And the king of Sodom went out to meet him after his return from the defeat of Chedorlaomer and the kings who were with him, and Abram refused.

ref: and asked, “Hagar, where have you come from, and where are you going?” She answered, “I'm running away from Sarai, my owner.”
text: Ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Hagar, Sarai weyō, nẽgbe̱ odše̱, ni nẽgbe̱ hũ oyaä? Ni ekē̱: Miwura Sarai hĩe midšoo̱ foi ye̱ ne̱.
translated: And he said to her: Hagar, Sarai's maidservant, where have you come from and where are you going? And she said: I am fleeing from my mistress Sarai.

ref: Hagar thought, “Have I really seen God and lived to tell about it?” So from then on she called him, “The God Who Sees Me.”
text: Ni etšē̱ Iehowa ni ke̱le̱ wie le̱ gbe̱i ake̱: Bo dši Mawu ni naämi! Edšake̱ ekē̱: Ani bie̱ ne̱ hũ mikwe̱ moni naämi le̱ yi se̱ lo?
translated: They said to Jehovah that they would not listen to him again: Because God has forsaken us! Therefore they said: Why should we listen to words that we do not understand?

ref: I will keep my solemn promise to you and give you more descendants than can be counted.”
text: Ni mike̱ mikpãṅmo̱ le̱ aṅmẽ mike̱o teṅ, ni mahã oyi afá babaō dientše̱.
translated: When we were still young, we used to go to the farm with our father.

ref: Abraham, you and all future members of your family must promise to obey me.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ sā kē̱ Abraham ake̱: Ši bo hũ, ye mikpãṅmo̱ le̱ no̱, bo ke̱ osešibii ni aaba ye̱ ose̱ le̱ ye̱ ame̱yino̱i le̱ amli.
translated: God said to Abraham: Take your son, your only son whom you love, and go to the land of Moriah, and offer him there as a burnt offering.

ref: Abraham, from now on your wife's name will be Sarah instead of Sarai.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ sã kē̱ Abraham ake̱: Oṅã Sarai le̱, kātšé̱ egbe̱i ake̱ Sarai doṅṅ, ši Sara dši egbe̱i.
translated: And God said to Abraham: As for your wife Sarai, you shall not call her Sarai, but Sarah shall be her name.

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ kē̱: Le̱le̱ṅ, oṅã Sara aafo̱ binū ahão, ni oowole̱ gbe̱i ake̱ Isak; ni mahã mike̱le̱ kpãṅmo̱ le̱ amã ši, ni atšṍ *nãno̱ kpãṅmo̱ ahã éseši ni aaba ye̱ ese̱ le̱.
translated: And God said: No, but Sarah your wife shall bear you a son, and you shall call his name Isaac; and I will establish my covenant with him for an everlasting covenant for his seed after him.

ref: Now I am going down to see for myself if those people really are that bad. If they aren't, I would like to know.”
text: *makpleke ši mate agbe̱ne̱, ni †mayakwe̱ ake̱, bo̱ni blo̱mo̱ le̱ ba miṅõ le̱, ame̱fe nakai kwrā lo; ni ke̱-dšee nakai hũ, ni male. *
translated: when you have finished praying, do not be like the hypocrites, for they love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the street corners to be seen by others; truly I tell you, they have received their reward in full.

ref: That night they got their father drunk, and the older daughter got in bed with him, but he was too drunk even to know she was there.
text: Ni ame̱hã ame̱tše̱ wein enu nakai nyō le̱ṅ; ni onukpa le̱ yiṅ te, ni eke̱ etše̱ yawo̱; ni elee ešikãmo̱ mli lo ešitē mli.
translated: And you shall not eat any blood, whether of fowl or of beast, in any of your dwellings.

ref: But God came to Abimelech in a dream and said, “You have taken a married woman into your home, and for this you will die!”
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ ba Abimelek ṅõ nyōṅ ye̱ lamõ mli, ni ekē̱le̱ ake̱: Na, obagbo ye̱ yō le̱ ní oṅõ le̱ hewo̱, edšake̱ nū ṅã dšile̱.
translated: And God came to Abimelech in a dream by night, and said to him: Behold, you are a dead man because of the woman you have taken, for she is a married woman.

ref: and told Abraham he could settle anywhere in his country.
text: Ni Abimelek kē̱: Na, mišikpoṅ le̱ kã ohĩe ne̱; hĩ heni osumoo̱.
translated: And Abimelech said: Behold, my land is before you; dwell where it pleases you.

ref: The Lord was good to Sarah and kept his promise.
text: Ni Iehowa basra Sara, take̱ bo̱ni ekē̱ le̱, ni Iehowa fe ehã Sara, take̱ bo̱ni ewie le̱.
translated: And Jehovah blessed Sarah, as he had said, and Jehovah did for Sarah, as he had promised.

ref: Although Abraham was very old, Sarah had a son exactly at the time God had said.
text: Ni Sara ná mũsũ, ni eke̱ Abraham fo̱ binū ye̱ egbo̱le̱ mli, ye̱ be ni Nyoṅmo̱ ekē̱le̱ le̱ mli. Heb.
translated: Sarah was barren, and she was past the age of childbearing, but she believed what God said. Heb.

ref: but the son of the slave woman is also your son, and I will make his descendants into a great nation.”

translated: When God created man, he made him in his own image; he created them male and female, and he blessed them and named them mankind.

text: Ni eba mli ye̱ nakai be le̱ mli ake̱ Abimelek ke̱ etatše̱ Fikol bakē̱ Abraham ake̱: Nyoṅmo̱ ke̱o ye̱ ye̱ nibii fẽ ní ofeo̱ le̱ mli.
translated: At that time Abimelech and Phicol his army commander said to Abraham: God is with you in everything you do.

translated: And Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba, and called there on the name of the LORD, the everlasting God.

ref: Then Abraham lived a long time as a foreigner in the land of the Philistines.
text: Ni Abraham to gbo̱ ye̱ Filistibii le̱ ašikpoṅ le̱ no̱ gbĩĩ pī.

translated: And Abraham called the name of that place: Jehovah will provide. Therefore it is said to this day: In the mountain of Jehovah it shall be provided.

ref: But the servant asked, “What if the young woman I choose refuses to leave home and come here with me? Should I send Isaac there to look for a wife?”

translated: If someone invites you to a feast and you go, do not sit in the place of honor; perhaps someone more important than you has been invited.

text: Ni ekē̱: Nu, minuṅtšo̱! Ni ehe fe oyá ni eto egbé̱ le̱ ehĩe edẽ ni ehã enu.

ref: The Lord has been good to my master and has made him very rich. He has given him many sheep, goats, cattle, camels, and donkeys, as well as a lot of silver and gold, and many slaves.
text: Ni Iehowa edšō̱ minuṅtšo̱ naakpa, ni etšṍ gbomo agbo; ni ehãle̱ tōi ke̱ tšinai, ke̱ dſie̱te̱i ke̱ šika, ke̱ wehĩĩ ke̱ weyei, ke̱ yomai ke̱ tedšii.
translated: The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want. He makes me lie down in green pastures. He leads me beside still waters. He restores my soul. He leads me in paths of righteousness for his name's sake.

ref: Sarah, my master's wife, didn't have any children until she was very old. Then she had a son, and my master has given him everything.
text: Ni minuṅtšo̱ ṅã Sara le̱ ke̱ minuṅtšo̱ efo̱ binū ye̱ egbo̱le̱ se̱ ne̱; ni le̱ eṅõ enii fẽ ni eyo̱ le̱ ehã.
translated: And the months that Sarah lived were one hundred years and twenty years and seven years; these were the years of the life of Sarah.

ref: I solemnly promised my master that I would do what he said. And he told me, “Don't choose a wife for my son from the women in this land of Canaan.
text: Ni minuṅtšo̱ hã mikã kitã ake̱: Kaw̌e̱ Kanaanbii, mei ašikpoṅ no̱ ni miyo̱ ne̱ abiyei le̱ ateṅ moko obã mibi le̱,

text: Ni mikē̱ minuṅtšo̱ akē̱: Ekole̱ yō le̱ ebanyieṅ mise̱.
translated: I will tell you a story: There was a certain man who had two sons.

ref: My master answered, “I have always obeyed the Lord, and he will send his angel to help you find my son a wife from among my own relatives.
text: Ni ekē̱mi akē̱: Iehowa, *mo hĩe ní minyie ne̱, aatšũ ebo̱fo afata ohe ke̱-ya, ni eehã ogbe̱ le̱ adša, ni oyaw̌e̱ yō ye̱ midšaku ke̱ mitše̱ we lē̱ obahã mibi le̱. *
translated: And I said: O Jehovah, you yourself have said to me, 'Buy for yourself the field for money and take witnesses,' whereas the city has been given into the hand of the Chaldeans.

ref: When I came to the well today, I silently prayed, “You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships, so please lead me to a wife for his son
text: Ni ṅme̱ne̱ miba nubu le̱ he, ni mikē̱: Iehowa, minuṅtšo̱ Abraham Nyoṅmo̱! ke̱dši oobã migbe̱ ni minyie ne̱ adša le̱,
translated: And when I had not yet finished speaking, behold, Rebekah came out with her pitcher on her shoulder; and she went down to the well and drew water. And I said to her, Please let me drink.

ref: I asked her who her father was, and she answered, “My father is Bethuel the son of Nahor and Milcah.” At once I put the ring in her nose and the bracelets on her arms.
text: Ni mibile̱ ake̱: Namo bi dšio? Ni ekē̱: Betuel, Nahor bi ni eke̱ Milka fo̱ le̱, bi dšimi. Ni miṅõ ga le̱ miwo egugõṅ, ni miṅõ ninešigai le̱ miwo enine;
translated: And they asked her: Whose daughter are you? And she said: Betuel, the son of Nahor whom Milka bore to him. And I gave her a nose ring, and I gave bracelets for her hands;

ref: Laban and Bethuel answered, “The Lord has done this. We have no choice in the matter.
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Laban ke̱ Betuel here no̱ ni ame̱kē̱: Iehowa ṅõ sane nē̱ dše̱; wo̱nyẽṅ wo̱ke̱o awie efõṅ lo ekpakpa.
translated: Then Laban and Bethuel answered and said: This thing comes from the Lord; we cannot speak to you bad or good.

ref: The servant told Isaac everything that had happened.
text: Ni tšũlo̱ le̱ gba Isak sãdši fẽ ní eyaye le̱.
translated: And the well that Isaac dug is there until today.

ref: Midian also had five sons: Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah.

translated: And the sons of Midian were Ephah, and Epher, and Hanoch, and Abida, and Eldaah. All these were the children of Keturah.

ref: with his beautiful wife Rebekah. He was afraid that someone might kill him to get her, and so he told everyone that Rebekah was his sister.
text: Ni dše̱ibii le̱ bi eṅã le̱ ši; ni ekē̱ akē̱: Minyemiyō ni; edšakē̱ ēše gbeyei ake̱ eekē̱ ake̱: Miṅã ni, ekē̱: Beni afe ni dše̱ibii le̱ akabagbemi ye̱ Rebeka hewo̱, edšake̱ ebe ye̱ fe̱o.
translated: And they called her and asked her; and she said: I will go; and her brother and her mother answered and said: Let the girl stay with us a few days, at least ten; after that she shall go. But he said to them: Do not hinder me, seeing the Lord has prospered my way; send me away that I may go to my master.

translated: They are the families of the Levites; Moses counted them, and their males, and their children who were males.

ref: After the death of the king of Egypt, the Israelites still complained because they were forced to be slaves. They cried out for help,
text: Ni eba mli ake̱ nakai gbĩĩ pī le̱ amli le̱ Mizraim maṅtše̱ le̱ bagbo, ni Israelbii le̱ dõmo̱ ntšo̱i ye̱ nyṍṅyeli le̱ hewo, ni ame̱fó ame̱tšé̱ Nyoṅmo̱; ni ame-nyṍṅyeli le̱ mli fómo̱-ke̱-tšémo̱ le̱ *yase̱ Nyoṅmo̱ ṅõ. *
translated: And they will know that I am the LORD their God, who brought them out of Egypt so that I might dwell among them. I am the LORD their God.

ref: and God heard their loud cries. He did not forget the promise he had made to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob,
text: Ni Nyoṅmo̱ nu ame̱ṅke̱mo̱yeli blo̱mo̱ le̱, ni Nyoṅmo̱ kai *ekpãṅmo̱ ní eke̱ Abraham ke̱ Isak ke̱ Iakob kpãṅ le̱; *
translated: I am the God of your fathers, I am the God who appeared to Abraham and Isaac and Jacob;

ref: Call together the leaders of Israel and tell them that the God who was worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob has appeared to you. Tell them I have seen how terribly they are being treated in Egypt,
text: Ya ní oyabua Israel onukpai le̱ anā, ni okē̱ame̱ ake̱: Iehowa, nyetše̱mei a-Nyoṅmo̱ le̱, ní dši Abraham ke̱ Isak ke̱ Iakob Nyoṅmo̱ le̱, edšie ehe kpo etšõmi, ni ekē̱: Sramo̱ mibasranye, ni mina noni afeo̱nye ye̱ Mizraim le̱;
translated: And now, O Israel, hear the statutes and the judgments which I teach you, to do them; that ye may live, and go in and possess the land which the LORD, the God of your fathers, giveth you.

ref: The Lord sent Aaron to meet Moses in the desert. So Aaron met Moses at Mount Sinai and greeted him with a kiss.
text: Ni Iehowa kē̱ Aaron ake̱: Ya ní oke̱ Mose ayakpe ye̱ ṅã le̱ no̱. Ni ete ní eke̱le̱ yakpe ye̱ Nyoṅmo̱ goṅ le̱ he, ni eſo̱ enā.
translated: And the Lord said to Aaron: Go and meet Moses in the wilderness. And he went and met him in the mountain of God, and kissed him.

ref: The king said, “Moses and Aaron, why are you keeping these people from working? Look how many you are keeping from doing their work. Now everyone get back to work!”
text: Ni Farao sā kē̱: Na, agbe̱ne̱ maṅ le̱ *yi efa momo ye̱ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, ni nyehã ame̱dšo̱ö̱ amehe noṅṅ ne̱! *
translated: And Pharaoh said: No, let only the men go and serve the Lord, for that is what you desire!

ref: That same day the king gave orders to his Egyptian slave bosses and to the Israelite men directly in charge of the Israelite slaves. He told them:
text: Ni nakai gbĩ le̱ no̱ noṅṅ le̱ Farao wo maṅ le̱ kplalo̱i ke̱ ame̱no̱kwe̱lo̱i le̱ akitã ake̱:
translated: And Pharaoh's heart was hardened, and he did not listen to them, just as the Lord had said.

ref: Don't give the slaves any more straw to put in their bricks. Force them to find their own straw wherever they can,
text: Nyekahãa maṅ le̱ dſe̱i ame̱ṅõ-fe ntayāi le̱ doṅṅ tamo̱ tšutšu le̱; ame̱dientše̱ ameya ní ame̱yabuabua dſe̱i anā ame̱hã amehe;
translated: The elders who rule well are to be considered worthy of double honor, especially those who work hard at preaching and teaching;

ref: Make them work so hard that they won't have time to listen to these lies.
text: Ahã nitšumo̱ le̱ anyẽ gbomei le̱, koni ato̱ame̱ ní ame̱kabo yakawiemo̱i atoi!
translated: The person who does not know the path should follow the one who knows it!

ref: The bosses beat the men in charge of the slaves and said, “Why didn't you force the slaves to make as many bricks yesterday and today as they did before?”
text: Ni ayi Israelbii le̱ ano̱kwe̱lo̱i ní Farao kplalo̱i le̱ ṅõ-to ame̱no̱ le̱ ake̱: Mẽhewo̱ ni nyè ke̱ ṅme̱ne̱ fẽ nyegbee nyentayāi ní atonye le̱ anā tamo̱ sá le̱?
translated: The Israelites also complained to Pharaoh saying: Why do you no longer give your servants straw to make bricks as before?

ref: Finally, the men in charge of the slaves went to the king and said, “Why are you treating us like this?
text: Ke̱ke̱ ni Israelbii le̱ ano̱kwe̱lo̱i le̱ ba ni ame̱bablo̱ ame̱wo Farao ame̱kē̱: Mẽhewo̱ oke̱ otšũdši yeo̱ ne̱ke̱?
translated: The elders of Israel went and told Pharaoh what God had said: When will you let my people go?

ref: Then the men said, “We hope the Lord will punish both of you for making the king and his officials hate us. Now they even have an excuse to kill us.” The Lord's Promise to Moses
text: ni ame̱kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Iehowa aakwe̱ ní ekodšo, edšake̱ nyehã wohe *edše fũ ewo Farao ke̱ etšũdši le̱, ni nyeṅõ klante nyewo ame̱dẽ ní ame̱ke̱-gbewo̱. *
translated: and you shall say to them: Jehovah has sent me to you, and he has told me all that I should say to Pharaoh, and all the signs that I should do before you.

ref: But the Lord sent Aaron and Moses with a message for the Israelites and for the king. He also ordered Aaron and Moses to free the people from Egypt. Family Record of Aaron and Moses
text: Ni Iehowa ke̱ Mose ke̱ Aaron wie, ni efã́ame̱ ake̱ ameya Israelbii le̱ ke̱ Mizraim maṅtše̱ Farao le̱ ṅõ, ní ame̱ya-dšie Israelbii le̱ ye̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ ne̱.
translated: The Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron, and gave them a command concerning the Israelites and Pharaoh king of Egypt, to bring the Israelites out of the land of Egypt.

ref: Merari's sons were Mahli and Mushi. All of the above were from the Levi tribe.
text: Ni Merari bii ne̱: Maheli ke̱ Muši. Mene̱mei dši Levi wekui le̱ ye̱ ame̱fo̱mo̱i le̱ anā.
translated: The sons of Merari were: Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites according to their fathers' houses.

ref: Korah's sons were Assir, Elkanah, and Abiasaph.
text: Ni Kora bii ne̱: Asir ke̱ Elkana ke̱ Abiasaf. Mene̱mei dši Korabii wekui le̱.
translated: These are the sons of Korah: Assir, Elkanah, and Abiasaph. These are the families of the Korahites.

ref: The Lord had commanded Aaron and Moses to lead every family and tribe of Israel out of Egypt,
text: Mene̱mei dši Aaron ke̱ Mose ni Iehowa kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Nyeyadšiea Israelbii le̱ ye̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ ye̱ ame̱tai le̱ anā le̱.
translated: The number of the children of Israel is the same as when they came out of Egypt.

ref: he said, “I am the Lord. Tell the king of Egypt everything I say to you.”
text: ake̱ Iehowa kē̱ Mose ake̱: Midši Iehowa le̱; kē̱mo̱ Farao, Mizraim maṅtše̱ le̱, nii fẽ ní mike̱o wieo̱ le̱.
translated: And the Lord said to Moses: Rise up early in the morning, and stand before Pharaoh, the king of Egypt, and say to him.

ref: The king behaved just as the Lord had said and stubbornly refused to listen. The Nile River Turns into Blood
text: Ni awadše Farao tšui, ni ebooame̱ toi, take̱ bo̱ni Iehowa *ewie le̱. *
translated: And Pharaoh's heart was hardened, and he did not listen to them, just as the LORD had said.

ref: Tomorrow morning take the stick that turned into a snake, then wait beside the Nile River for the king.
text: Ya Farao ṅõ lebi; na, ēdše kpo eya fā le̱ nā, ni oyadamo̱ fā le̱ nā omẽle̱, ni tšo le̱ ní tšṍ onũfũ le̱ ko̱ ohĩe odẽ,
translated: Pharaoh's heart was hardened; and he did not let the children of Israel go, nor did he release them from their bondage, nor did he stop his evil deeds against them,

ref: “The Lord is going to do something to show you that he really is the Lord. I will strike the Nile with this stick, and the water will turn into blood.
text: Boni Iehowa kē̱ ne̱: Ene̱ oke̱-ale ake̱ midši Iehowa le̱: na, mike̱ tšo ní mihĩe ye̱ midẽ ne̱ batſa fā le̱ mli nu le̱, ni *eetšṍ lá. *
translated: Thus says the Lord: Behold, I am against you, says the Lord: and I will draw my sword out of its sheath, and will cut off from you the righteous and the wicked.

ref: the fish died, and the water smelled so bad that none of the Egyptians could drink it. Blood was everywhere in Egypt.
text: Ni lōi ní yo̱ fā le̱ mli le̱ gboi, ni fā le̱ ša, ni Mizraimbii le̱ nyẽẽ fā le̱ mli nu le̱ anu; ni lá so̱ṅṅ yo̱ Mizraim šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ fẽ.
translated: And the people cried to Pharaoh for bread, and Pharaoh said to all the Egyptians, Go to Joseph; what he says to you, do; and the famine was over all the face of Egypt.
